steps.title: Find the leading coefficient using vertex form
steps.content:
Vertex form: $$f(x) = A(x-4)^2 - 6$$
Substitute point (3,-8): $$-8 = A(3-4)^2 - 6$$
Simplify: $$-8 = A(1) - 6$$
Solve for A: $$A = -2$$

With A = -2, the y-intercept of f is $$a = f(0) = -2(0-4)^2 - 6 = -2·16 - 6 = -38$$
The y-intercept of 5f is $$b = 5·f(0) = 5a = 5·(-38) = -190$$
Positive difference: $$|b - a| = |-190 - (-38)| = |-152| = 152$$
The answer is 152.
steps.title: Compute y-intercepts and their difference
